incorrects.description: Student selected 'The difference': confusing the second step (comparing) with the first step (finding the combined total)

params.description: Strategy statement pulses to draw attention to the first step.

steps.text: The difference comes later. When we see 'combined,' we add first. Look at the strategy: what's the first thing we do?

incorrects.description: Student selected 'The largest number': misunderstanding that 'combined' signals addition, not identifying an extreme value

params.description: The word 'combined' highlights on the strategy statement.

steps.text: Combined means put together, not pick the biggest. When we see 'combined,' we add the categories. What do we get when we add?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation highlights 'Find the combined total first' on the strategy statement.

steps.text: Let me show you. Combined means add. When we see 'combined' or 'together,' we find the total by adding those categories. That's always the first step. The answer is the total.
